title: Fix jlcpcb CAD fallback for library footprints
description: Fixes 3D rendering for library footprints that do not provide a CAD model by falling back cleanly to a bounding box instead of surfacing a parser error.
body:
## What changed
This updates CAD fallback generation for library-style footprints so `cad_component.footprinter_string` is only emitted for direct footprinter strings such as `0402` or `soic8`.

For library references like `jlcpcb:C51950748`, KiCad library refs, URLs, and static asset paths, the fallback now emits a bounding-box CAD component instead of forwarding the library reference as a footprinter string.

## Why this changed
The 3D viewer was trying to parse `jlcpcb:...` as a raw footprinter function when no CAD model was available. That produced runtime errors like:

`Invalid footprint function, got "jlcpcb"`

The underlying footprint could still load for PCB rendering, but the CAD fallback path was sending an invalid string into the 3D engine.

## Impact
This fixes 3D rendering for library footprints that do not provide a CAD model by falling back cleanly to a bounding box instead of surfacing a parser error.

## Root cause
`NormalComponent.doInitialCadModelRender()` treated any string footprint as a valid `footprinter_string` fallback. Library refs such as `jlcpcb:...` are valid footprint lookup keys, but they are not valid raw footprinter expressions.

## Validation
- `bun test tests/footprint/footprint-library-map-jlcpcb-cadmodel.test.tsx`
- `bun test tests/components/normal-components/cad-component-bounding-box.test.tsx`

## Notes
Repo-wide build/type generation still has unrelated pre-existing DTS failures in `lib/components/primitive-components/PcbTrace.ts`.

title: fix: preserve full redirect URL (path, query, and hash) on login and session timeout
description: Fixes the issue where logging back in after a session timeout discards the users location state, search parameters, or hash fragments, ensuring users are redirected back to their intended location with full URL structure preserved.
body:
## Summary

Resolves the issue where logging back in after a session timeout (or direct access to protected paths) discards the user's location state, search parameters, or hash fragments (such as compressed playground code inside the `#data:` hash).

### Key Improvements:
1. **Dynamic Toast Location Capture**: Updated the session-expired toast handler in `src/hooks/use-axios.tsx` to read the location dynamically from the browser `window.location` at click-time. This eliminates the stale closure bug in the memoized toast block from past PRs (where `orgLoginRedirect` was stale when the user navigated).
2. **Protected Settings Preservation**:
   - `src/pages/package-settings.tsx` and `src/pages/user-settings.tsx` now preserve the full URL structure (`pathname + search + hash`) as a query parameter when bouncing unauthenticated users to `/login`.
3. **Robust Google/OAuth Re-authentication**:
   - Updated `src/pages/login.tsx` to extract the `redirect` query parameter and redirect the authenticated user back to their intended target.
4. **Open-Redirect Protection**:
   - Included a strict validation check (`redirectParam.startsWith("/")`) to prevent open-redirect vulnerabilities, ensuring only local relative redirects are allowed.

Closes #3023

---

## Test Plan / Walkthrough

### 1. Session Expiry Flow (Editor / Playground)
1. Log in to `tscircuit.com`.
2. Open any package or snippet editor containing a hash code block:
   `http://localhost:5173/editor?snippet_id=123#data:application/gzip;base64,...`
3. Simulate a session timeout:
   - Run in the browser console: `localStorage.clear()` (or clear cookies/session state).
   - Trigger a backend request to get a `401 Unauthorized`.
4. Click the **"Session Expired. Click to login"** toast that appears.
5. Verify you are navigated to `/login?redirect=...`.
6. Complete the login flow and verify you are redirected exactly back to the URL with the complete search query (`?snippet_id=123`) and hash fragment (`#data:...`) intact.

### 2. Direct Settings Access
1. Open an incognito browser window or log out.
2. Direct-navigate to:
   - `/settings`
   - `/<author>/<packageName>/settings`
3. Verify you are redirected to `/login?redirect=%2Fsettings` (or the respective package settings path).
4. Log in and verify you are redirected back to the correct settings page.

---

## Demonstration Video

Here is the recorded verification video demonstrating the session timeout bounce and direct settings redirects preserving full URL states (pathname, search query, and hash fragments):

https://github.com/itisrohit/tscircuit.com/releases/download/v0.0.1-assets/session_timeout_redirect_verification.mov

title: [codex] Sync package version with npm
description: Resets package version in package.json from 0.0.536 to 0.0.535 to match the currently published npm version and removes a trailing blank line in README.md.
body:
## Summary

- Reset `package.json` from `0.0.536` to `0.0.535` so the repo matches the currently published npm version.
- Regenerated docs per repository workflow; this only removed a trailing blank line in `README.md`.

## Context

The publish workflow had pushed git tags `v0.0.536`, `v0.0.537`, and `v0.0.538` even though npm publish failed. Those bad upstream tags have been deleted, leaving upstream tags synced through `v0.0.535`, which matches npm latest.

## Validation

- `bun scripts/generate-component-types.ts`
- `bun scripts/generate-manual-edits-docs.ts`
- `bun scripts/generate-readme-docs.ts`
- `bun scripts/generate-props-overview.ts`

title: fix: persist CadViewer engine and camera choices across remounts
description: Fixes a localStorage race condition in CadViewer that clobbered the users persisted engine and camera-type choices on every mount.
body:
## Summary

Fix a localStorage race in `CadViewer` that clobbered the user's persisted engine and camera-type choices on every mount.

**Root cause**

For both `engine` and `cameraType`, the component was using a pair of effects:
1. A read effect that loaded the stored value and called `setEngine` / `setCameraType`.
2. A write effect that mirrored current state into `localStorage`.

On mount, both effects fire after the first render. The write effect runs with the closure-captured initial state (`"manifold"` / `"perspective"`) and overwrites the stored value before the read effect's setter has a chance to commit. The persisted choice gets clobbered.

**Fix**

- `engine` is now lazy-initialized from `localStorage` directly in `useState`, so the first write effect already sees the correct value.
- `cameraType` lives in `CameraControllerContext`. Added an optional `initialCameraType` prop on the provider so `CadViewer` can synchronously read `localStorage` and pass it down — same lazy-init shape, just one layer up.
- Removed both now-redundant read effects.

`autoRotate` / `autoRotateUserToggled` were already correctly lazy-initialized; no change there. The `localStorage` write in `StepModel.tsx` is a content cache (no state mirroring), so it's not affected by this pattern.

## Test plan

- [x] `bunx tsc --noEmit` — clean
- [x] `bun run format:check` — clean
- [ ] Manual: pick JSCAD engine, reload page, verify it stays selected
- [ ] Manual: switch to orthographic camera, reload, verify it stays selected

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Add Native Gerber Aperture Support for Pill SMT Pads
description: This pull request introduces support for native Gerber apertures specifically for pill-shaped SMT pads. It includes validation for the dimensions of the pill shape and defines the necessary configurations for both standard and solder mask layers. The changes ensure that the Gerber output correctly represents pill-shaped pads, enhancing the overall functionality of the circuit design tool.

title: Use text geometry for anchor alignment
description: Replaces approximate text anchor offsets with geometry derived from actual glyph outline bounds, ensuring accurate text alignment and consistency in rendering knockout backgrounds and stroked text.
body:
## Summary
- replace approximate text anchor offsets with geometry derived from actual glyph outline bounds
- add `getTextGeometry` so stroke rendering and knockout rendering share the same line placements, glyph groups, and bounds
- refresh text-related regression snapshots to match the corrected descender and anchor placement behavior

## Why
Text anchoring was being calculated from layout approximations instead of the real rendered glyph outlines. That could shift bottom, center, and multiline alignment, especially for descenders and knockout text. Computing anchor positions from the actual glyph geometry keeps the requested anchor aligned with what is drawn.

## Impact
- text anchor alignment now follows the true rendered bounds
- knockout backgrounds and stroked text use the same geometry model, reducing mismatches
- the new exported geometry helper can be reused by other text rendering paths

## Validation
- `bun test` ✅ (136 passing)
- `bun run format:check` ✅
- `bunx tsc --noEmit` ⚠️ currently fails in unrelated existing `PcbNote*` / `pcb_panel` typing checks outside this branch's diff

title: Fix same-net routed traces being treated as obstacles in KRT
description: Fixes routing failure where same-net copper traces block later same-net traces in KRT.
body:
This fixes a KRT routing failure where previously routed copper on the same electrical net could block later same-net traces.

  The repro board failed on source_trace_12 because earlier routed traces on the same net were converted into phase obstacles carrying their own trace ids. KRT only compared obstacles against the current connection’s local ids, so same-net copper was incorrectly treated as foreign copper and blocked routing.

  The fix expands each connection into its full same-net id set using the connectedTo relationships already present in the SimpleRouteJson. Obstacle filtering now uses that same-net set for both static obstacles and previously routed cells, so same-net copper is connectable while other-net copper still blocks normally.

title: Add focused same-net routed trace obstacle repro
description: Adds a test to reproduce an autorouting error when traces are routed on the same net with obstacles.

title: Add breakout point spacing solver with clearer QFN snapshots
description: Computes a breakout boundary point for each inside port by projecting toward the outside target, avoids already-used boundary points when usedBoundaryPoints and boundaryPointSpacing are provided, chooses the nearest available point on the same boundary edge when the ideal point is occupied, and returns only breakoutPoints, not routed traces.
body:
- Computes a breakout boundary point for each inside port by projecting toward the outside target.
- Avoids already-used boundary points when usedBoundaryPoints and boundaryPointSpacing are provided.
- Chooses the nearest available point on the same boundary edge when the ideal point is occupied
- Returns only breakoutPoints, not routed traces.
